meaning: n.(绳等的)结,(树的)节,节(船速,＝哩／小时)v.打结
examples: She tied a knot in a cherry stem.
I tied a knot in the rope.
His clumsy fingers couldn't untie the knot.
But it was Riccardo Muti who eventually tied the knot last year after not a little persuasion.
In a 20-knot breeze, the steel-hulled yacht can easily accelerate past 14 knots, or 16mph.
